title: 10 best herbal tinctures for oily hair
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for oily hair are concentrated liquid extracts of herbs that are used to treat oily hair. They work by nourishing the scalp, balancing sebum production, and promoting a healthy hair growth cycle. Some examples of herbal tinctures that help with oily hair include rosemary, horsetail, nettle, and burdock. These herbs contain beneficial compounds such as antioxidants, anti-inflammatory agents, and nutrients that help to regulate oil production, soothe an irritated scalp, and strengthen hair follicles. By using herbal tinctures, individuals with oily hair can experience a reduction in excess oil, improved scalp health, and overall better hair appearance. This can lead to increased confidence, improved self-esteem, and a more positive self-image.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Rosemary tincture helps with oily hair because it has natural astringent properties that can help regulate sebum production in the scalp. Sebum is an oily substance that helps keep the hair and skin moisturized, but an overproduction of sebum can lead to oily hair and other scalp issues. The rosemary tincture helps to balance the scalp's natural oil production, leaving the hair looking and feeling cleaner for longer periods. Additionally, rosemary tincture has anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help soothe and protect the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the likelihood of dandruff and other scalp irritations.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Rosemary tincture helps to regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing excess oiliness in hair. This property is due to the presence of tannins and volatile oils such as cineole and camphor.

Antimicrobial: It inhibits the growth of microorganisms on the scalp, preventing dandruff and other related issues. The main active constituents responsible for this property are caffeic acid, rosemarinic acid, and carnosol.

Anti-inflammatory: Rosemary tincture reduces inflammation and irritation on the scalp, promoting a healthy environment for hair growth. The anti-inflammatory effects are attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid and carnosol.
Circulation stimulant: It improves blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing oiliness. The active constituents responsible for this property include ursolic acid and carnosic acid.

Antioxidant: Rosemary tincture protects the scalp and hair from environmental stressors and damage, maintaining overall hair health. The main antioxidant compounds found in rosemary are carnosol, carnosic acid, and rosmarinic acid.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the rosemary plant are rich in essential oils and antioxidants, making them ideal for creating medicinal tinctures that can help regulate sebum production in the scalp and promote hair growth.

Flowers: The flowers of the rosemary plant contain high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, making them useful for creating tinctures that can help soothe an itchy scalp and reduce inflammation associated with oily hair.
Stems: The stems of the rosemary plant contain beneficial compounds that can help stimulate blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing excess oil production.

Seeds: The seeds of the rosemary plant are rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, making them useful for creating tinctures that can help nourish and strengthen the hair follicles, reducing breakage and promoting healthy, shiny hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality fresh or dried rosemary leaves and stems.
Combine 20% rosemary material with 80% high-proof vodka in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily to ensure thorough extraction.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another clean glass container.
Store the finished tincture in a dark glass bottle at room temperature for up to 2 years.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sage tincture helps with oily hair because it contains powerful astringent and antibacterial properties. These properties help to regulate sebum production, reducing the excess oil that can weigh down hair and make it appear greasy. Sage is also known for its ability to stimulate hair growth and improve scalp circulation, promoting healthier and more balanced hair. Its natural antioxidants also help to protect the hair from environmental damage and keep it looking vibrant and healthy. By using sage tincture regularly, you can effectively manage oily hair and enjoy a cleaner, fresher, and more manageable hairstyle.
remedy_properties: Sebum regulation: Sage tincture helps normalize sebum production, addressing the root cause of oily hair. This is due to the presence of camphor and tannins, which have astringent and drying effects on the scalp.

Antimicrobial properties: Sage tincture inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, maintaining a healthy scalp environment. The active constituents responsible for this effect include caryophyllene, α-humulene, and rosmarinic acid.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Sage tincture reduces scalp inflammation, which can contribute to excess oil production. This is attributed to the presence of rosmarinic acid, luteolin, and apigenin.

Antioxidant properties: Sage tincture protects the scalp from oxidative stress, promoting overall hair health. This is due to the presence of various phenolic acids, such as rosmarinic acid and caffeic acid.

Circulation improvement: Sage tincture enhances blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing oiliness. This is facilitated by the active constituents ursolic acid and carnosic acid, which have vasodilatory effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in volatile oils, the leaves are used to create tinctures that help regulate sebum production in the scalp.
Flowers: Containing flavonoids and essential oils, sage flowers are used in tinctures to soothe and balance oily hair and scalp.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and fatty acids, sage seeds are used in tinctures to nourish the hair and control oil production.
Stems: With astringent properties, sage stems are included in tinctures to help reduce excess oil and tighten the scalp.
Roots: Packed with vitamins and minerals, the roots are used in tinctures to strengthen hair, promote hair growth, and control oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality salvia officinalis leaves and stems free from contaminants or mold.
Chop the fresh herbs into small pieces to increase surface area for extraction.
Combine 10 parts of chopped sage with 20 parts of organic vodka in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for 6 weeks allowing the mixture to macerate and extract.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle discarding solids.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Oregano tincture helps with oily hair because it has antifungal and antibacterial properties that can help regulate the production of sebum, the oil produced by the scalp. Oregano's natural compounds, such as thymol and carvacrol, can help to balance the scalp's natural oils and reduce excess oiliness. Additionally, oregano tincture can help to soothe inflammation and irritation on the scalp, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing the likelihood of oily hair. Regular use of oregano tincture can help to maintain a balanced scalp and promote healthy, shiny, and manageable hair.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Oregano tincture's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of microorganisms that can contribute to an oily scalp, reducing the risk of dandruff and irritation. This is due to the presence of carvacrol and thymol.
Sebum regulation: Oregano tincture helps regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil on the scalp, thanks to the active constituents, terpinen-4-ol and γ-terpinene.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties alleviate scalp irritation and inflammation caused by excess sebum and bacteria, promoting a healthier scalp and hair. This is attributed to the compound, β-caryophyllene.

Antioxidant: Oregano tincture's high antioxidant content protects the scalp and hair from oxidative stress and damage, which can lead to an oily scalp and hair loss. The antioxidant properties come from rosmarinic acid and various flavonoids.

Circulation enhancement: Oregano tincture improves blood circulation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing oiliness by ensuring proper nutrient distribution and waste removal. This is facilitated by the essential oil components, including ρ-cymene and limonene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and essential oils, oregano leaves help regulate sebum production in the scalp.
Flowers: Containing therapeutic compounds, oregano flowers can balance oiliness and promote hair health.
Seeds: High in nutrients and volatile oils, oregano seeds can help reduce scalp inflammation and control oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried oregano vulgare leaves and stems for this recipe.
Chop 2 cups of fresh oregano vulgare leaves and stems into small pieces for infusion.
Combine chopped oregano vulgare with 4 cups of high-proof grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the mixture in a cool dark place for 6 to 8 weeks for maceration and extraction.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Thyme tincture helps with oily hair because it has antibacterial and antifungal properties that can help regulate the production of sebum on the scalp. Sebum is a natural oil that keeps the hair and scalp moisturized, but an overproduction of sebum can lead to oily hair. Thyme tincture can help balance the scalp's natural oil production, leaving hair looking and feeling cleaner for longer periods of time. Additionally, thyme has astringent properties that can help tone and tighten the scalp, reducing the appearance of oily hair. Regular use of thyme tincture can help promote a healthy scalp and hair, while also leaving hair looking and feeling fresh and revitalized.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Thyme tincture's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of microorganisms that can contribute to an oily scalp, as it contains active compounds like thymol and carvacrol.
Astringent: Its astringent properties help to balance the production of sebum on the scalp, reducing excess oiliness, due to the presence of tannins.

Anti-inflammatory: Thyme tincture's anti-inflammatory effects help soothe irritation and reduce inflammation on the scalp, providing relief for those with oily hair, thanks to compounds like rosmarinic acid.
Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of thyme tincture help protect the scalp from oxidative stress and damage, promoting healthier hair growth, as it contains flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Circulation-boosting: Thyme tincture can help improve blood flow to the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing oiliness by carrying nutrients to the hair follicles and removing waste products, due to its essential oil content.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Thyme leaves are rich in essential oils that help regulate sebum production, making them ideal for treating oily hair.
Flowers: Thyme flowers contain antiseptic and astringent properties that help balance the scalp's oil production and promote healthy hair growth.
Stems: Thyme stems have similar properties to leaves and flowers, and they can be used to make medicinal tinctures that help control oily hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh thymus vulgaris leaves in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Weigh 100 grams of fresh thyme leaves and chop them coarsely using sharp scissors or a knife.
Combine chopped thyme with 500 milliliters of 95% ethanol vodka in a clean glass jar, ensuring all herb is submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool dark place for 14 days to allow maceration and extraction.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into another glass container, discarding solids and retaining clear tincture liquid.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with oily hair because it has antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties that can help regulate sebum production in the scalp. The tincture can help soothe an irritated scalp, unclog hair follicles, and promote hair growth. Additionally, lavender has natural cleansing properties that can remove excess oil and build-up from the hair, leaving it looking and feeling clean and refreshed. By using lavender tincture regularly, it can help balance the natural oils in the hair and scalp, reducing the appearance of oily hair and promoting healthier, more manageable hair.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: English lavender tincture's antiseptic properties help control the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to an oily scalp. This is important for oily hair because it helps maintain a healthy scalp environment and reduces excess oil production. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool, eucalyptol, and camphor.

Astringent: Lavender tincture has astringent properties that help tighten the scalp's pores, reducing excess sebum production. This is important for oily hair because it helps regulate oil secretion and improves hair texture. The primary active constituent responsible for this property is tannin.

Balancing sebum production: English lavender tincture helps balance sebum production on the scalp, reducing oiliness. This is important for oily hair because it helps maintain the natural moisture level of the hair and prevents it from becoming too greasy. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalyl acetate and geraniol.

Anti-inflammatory: Lavender tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that help soothe and calm inflamed scalps. This is important for oily hair because it reduces redness, itching, and irritation that can be associated with an oily scalp. The active constituents responsible for this property include linalool and caryophyllene.

Circulation-boosting: English lavender tincture promotes blood circulation in the scalp, which can help regulate oil production and improve hair health. This is important for oily hair because it ensures the scalp receives proper nutrients and promotes healthier hair growth. The active constituents responsible for this property include eucalyptol and camphor.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in essential oils, lavender flowers can help regulate sebum production in the scalp, reducing oily hair.
Leaves: Containing therapeutic compounds, lavender leaves can help balance scalp oils and have a calming effect on the skin.
Seeds: High in fatty acids, lavender seeds can moisturize and nourish the hair, promoting a healthy scalp and reducing oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh english lavender flowers, choosing blooms in their prime for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested flowers gently with cold water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one part dried english lavender flowers with five parts 95% grain alcohol in a clean glass jar.
Store the jar in a cool dark place for eight weeks, shaking daily for maximum extraction.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth and discard the solids, transferring the tincture to dark glass bottles.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint tincture helps with oily hair because it has antiseptic and antibacterial properties that can help to balance the scalp's natural oil production. It can also help to unclog hair follicles, promoting hair growth and reducing excess oil. Peppermint's natural cooling and soothing effects can also help to relieve an itchy or irritated scalp, which can be a common issue for those with oily hair. Additionally, peppermint tincture can help to increase blood flow to the scalp, which can stimulate hair growth and improve the overall health of the hair.
remedy_properties: Antiseptic: Peppermint tincture's antiseptic properties help reduce scalp inflammation and prevent the buildup of excess oil and bacteria that can lead to oily hair. This is due to the presence of menthol and menthone.

Astringent: Peppermint tincture has astringent properties that help regulate sebum production, reducing the amount of oil on the scalp and hair. The active constituents responsible for this are tannins and flavonoids.

Anti-inflammatory: Peppermint tincture's anti-inflammatory properties soothe an irritated scalp, reducing redness and itching, which can contribute to excess oil production. This is attributed to rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
Circulation-boosting: Peppermint tincture improves blood circulation in the scalp, promoting hair growth and reducing oil production. The main active constituent responsible for this is menthol.

Balancing: Peppermint tincture helps balance the pH levels of the scalp, which can help regulate oil production and maintain a healthy scalp. This is due to the combined effects of the various active constituents, including menthol, menthone, and flavonoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in menthol and menthone, peppermint leaves can help balance sebum production in the scalp, reducing oily hair.
Flowers: Containing essential oils, peppermint flowers can help promote hair growth and soothe itchy, oily scalps.
Seeds: High in antioxidants and fatty acids, peppermint seeds can help nourish and strengthen oily hair, while promoting a healthy scalp.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a well-identified mentha x piperita plant, selecting young leaves with high essential oil content.
Clean and dry the harvested leaves thoroughly by gently shaking off excess moisture and spreading them out in a single layer.
Combine 200g of dried peppermint leaves with 1000ml of high-proof vodka or grain alcohol in a clean glass jar, ensuring the herb material is fully submerged.
Allow the mixture to macerate for 6-8 weeks, shaking the jar every 2-3 days to facilitate extraction and prevent settling, then strain and filter the liquid.
Transfer the resulting tincture to dark glass bottles and store in a cool, dry place for up to 2 years, using 20-30 drops as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Narrow-leaved paperbark tincture helps with oily hair because it contains natural anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help regulate sebum production in the scalp. The herb's active compounds, such as terpenoids and flavonoids, can penetrate the hair follicles and control the overproduction of oil, leaving the hair looking clean and refreshed. Additionally, the tincture can help soothe an itchy and irritated scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing the appearance of greasy hair. With regular use, narrow-leaved paperbark tincture can help restore the natural balance of the scalp and improve the overall health of the hair.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Narrow-leaved paperbark tincture's anti-inflammatory property helps reduce scalp inflammation caused by excess sebum production, which is important for oily hair as it can alleviate irritation and promote a healthier scalp. The active constituents responsible for this property include terpenoids and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial property of narrow-leaved paperbark tincture helps control the growth of microorganisms on the scalp, preventing potential infections and maintaining a balanced scalp microbiome, which is essential for managing oily hair. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are tannins and essential oils.

Astringent: Narrow-leaved paperbark tincture's astringent property helps regulate sebum production by tightening the pores and reducing excess oil secretion, making it an important property for managing oily hair. The active constituents responsible for this astringency include tannins and saponins.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant property of narrow-leaved paperbark tincture helps protect the scalp and hair follicles from oxidative stress and damage caused by free radicals, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing the likelihood of oily hair. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Circulation-enhancing: Narrow-leaved paperbark tincture's ability to improve blood circulation in the scalp ensures that essential nutrients are delivered to the hair follicles, promoting hair growth and reducing excess sebum production, which is important for managing oily hair. The active constituents responsible for this property include essential oils and terpenoids.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants, leaves help balance scalp oil production and soothe irritation.
Bark: Contains anti-inflammatory properties, which can help regulate sebum production and reduce scalp inflammation.
Seeds: Seeds are a great source of essential fatty acids, promoting hair growth and nourishing the scalp to reduce excess oil.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried leaves of melaleuca alternifolia in quantities according to the desired yield and quality standards.
Chop the collected leaves into smaller pieces and combine with vodka or grain alcohol at a ratio of 1:4.
Transfer the leaf-alcohol mixture to a clean glass jar with a tight-fitting lid and store it in a dark place for 4-6 weeks.
After the maceration period, strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into another clean glass container discarding the solids.
Store the tincture in a cool, dark place at room temperature away from direct sunlight and use within 2 years after preparation.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Tasmanian blue gum tincture helps with oily hair because it contains powerful anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties that can help regulate sebum production in the scalp. The tincture is derived from the leaves of the Tasmanian blue gum tree, which contains high levels of tannins, a type of polyphenolic compound that can help to tighten pores and reduce oiliness. Additionally, the tincture can help to soothe an itchy or irritated scalp, which can often be a contributing factor to excess oil production. By using Tasmanian blue gum tincture regularly, individuals with oily hair can expect to see a reduction in oiliness and a healthier, more balanced scalp.
remedy_properties: Antimicrobial: Tasmanian blue gum tincture's antimicrobial properties help control the growth of microorganisms on the scalp that can lead to excess oil production. The active constituents responsible for this property include terpinen-4-ol and α-pinene.

Anti-inflammatory: The anti-inflammatory properties of Tasmanian blue gum tincture help reduce scalp inflammation, which can contribute to excessive sebum production. The active constituents responsible for this property include γ-terpinene and rutin.

Astringent: Tasmanian blue gum tincture has astringent properties that help regulate sebum production and tighten pores, reducing the appearance of oily hair. The active constituents responsible for this property include gallic acid and ellagic acid.

Antioxidant: The antioxidant properties of Tasmanian blue gum tincture help protect the scalp from oxidative stress, promoting a healthy scalp environment and reducing excess oil production. The active constituents responsible for this property include quercetin and kaempferol.

Circulation-boosting: Tasmanian blue gum tincture helps improve blood circulation in the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing excess oil production. The active constituents responsible for this property include 1,8-cineole and limonene.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, leaves help regulate sebum production and soothe irritated scalps.
Bark: Contains tannins that can help absorb excess oil and provide astringent properties for balancing oily hair.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and nutrients, seeds can moisturize and nourish the scalp, promoting healthier hair growth and reducing oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh eucalyptus globulus leaves and stems in the morning after dew has dried for optimal potency.
Dry the harvested plant material using a food dehydrator on low heat for 6-8 hours or by air drying for several days.
Chop the dried plant material into small pieces to increase surface area for efficient extraction.
Combine the chopped plant material with 60% ethanol in a clean glass jar and store it in a cool dark place for 2 weeks, shaking daily, to allow for solvent extraction.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, discarding the solids, and label it for future use after filtering has been completed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eureka tincture helps with oily hair because it is formulated with a blend of natural herbs known for their ability to balance sebum production. The tincture works by penetrating the hair follicles and regulating the oil glands, thereby reducing the excess oil that can weigh down hair and give it a greasy appearance. By using Eureka tincture regularly, you can expect to see a reduction in oiliness, resulting in hair that looks and feels cleaner for longer periods of time. Additionally, the tincture can help to soothe and calm the scalp, reducing irritation and promoting healthier, more manageable hair.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Eureka tincture's astringent properties help to regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil on the scalp. This is due to the presence of tannins and flavonoids.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of eureka tincture help to maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the overgrowth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oily hair. The active constituents responsible for this property include alkaloids and terpenes.

Anti-inflammatory: Eureka tincture's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce scalp inflammation, which can be a contributing factor to oily hair. This is due to the presence of flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Circulation-boosting: The circulation-boosting properties of eureka tincture help to improve blood flow to the scalp, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oiliness. This is due to the presence of essential oils and saponins.

Balancing: Eureka tincture's balancing properties help to restore the natural pH level of the scalp, reducing oiliness and promoting healthy hair. This is due to the presence of organic acids and polyphenols.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain compounds that can help regulate sebum production, making it effective for treating oily hair.
Leaves: Rich in nutrients and antioxidants that can help promote hair growth and reduce excess oil.
Flowers: Have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an itchy scalp and help control oil production.
Seeds: Contain essential fatty acids that can help nourish the scalp and hair, promoting healthy hair growth and reducing oiliness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh citrus limon peels by cutting them into thin strips without any white pith remaining.
Dry the peel strips for 7-10 days in a warm dry place or using a food dehydrator at 60°c.
Combine the dried peel strips with 70% vodka to achieve a peel-to-alcohol ratio of 1:2 by weight.
Steep the mixture for 14-21 days in a glass jar, shaking daily and allowing the tincture to macerate slowly.
Filter the tincture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into dark glass bottles for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Key lime tincture helps with oily hair because it contains properties that can help regulate sebum production. The natural acids found in key limes can help balance the pH levels of the scalp, reducing excess oiliness. Additionally, key lime tincture has antimicrobial and antifungal properties that can help maintain a healthy scalp and prevent the buildup of dirt and oil. Regular use of key lime tincture can help control oily hair, leaving it looking and feeling clean and refreshed.
remedy_properties: Astringent: Key lime tincture has astringent properties that help regulate sebum production, reducing excess oil on the scalp. This is due to the high concentration of limonene and citric acid present in key limes.

Antimicrobial: Its antimicrobial properties help maintain a healthy scalp by preventing the growth of bacteria and fungi that can contribute to oily hair. The limonene and citric acid content contribute to these antimicrobial effects.

Exfoliating: Key lime tincture can gently exfoliate the scalp, removing dead skin cells and buildup that can clog hair follicles and cause excess oil production. The natural acids, such as citric acid, in key limes provide this exfoliating effect.

Balancing pH: The balanced pH of key lime tincture helps maintain the natural pH level of the scalp, promoting a healthier scalp environment and reducing excess oil production. This is due to the presence of natural acids like citric acid in key limes.

Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties can help soothe and reduce irritation on the scalp, which can lead to less oil production. The flavonoids and limonoids present in key limes contribute to these anti-inflammatory effects.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of the key lime plant are rich in nutrients and contain natural oils that can help regulate sebum production, making them ideal for treating oily hair.
Seeds: Key lime seeds are a great source of antioxidants and fatty acids, which can help nourish the scalp and promote healthy hair growth, while also reducing excess oil.
Bark: The bark of the key lime tree contains bioactive compounds that can help balance the scalp's natural oil production, making it an effective ingredient for treating oily hair.
Flowers: Key lime flowers have anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe an irritated scalp and help regulate oil production, resulting in healthier, more balanced hair.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh key lime leaves measuring 30-40 grams.
Rinse the leaves with filtered water to remove any dirt or debris.
Chop the leaves coarsely and combine with 100 proof vodka at a ratio of 1:4.
Steep the mixture in a clean glass jar for 2 weeks with occasional shaking.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle and store.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat oily hair with herbal tinctures is by using a mixture of rosemary and nettle. These herbs help regulate sebum production, reducing oiliness. Mix equal parts of rosemary and nettle tinctures with water, then apply the solution to your scalp and hair. Massage gently, let it sit for 10-15 minutes, then rinse thoroughly. For best results, use this treatment 2-3 times a week. Remember to always perform a patch test before applying any new product to your skin or hair.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to oily hair that are treated with herbal tinctures include dandruff, itchy scalp, and hair loss. These conditions can be caused by an imbalance in the natural oils produced by the scalp. Herbal tinctures, such as those made from rosemary, sage, and nettle, can help to regulate oil production, soothe inflammation, and promote hair growth. For example, rosemary tincture has been shown to increase circulation to the scalp, improving hair thickness and growth. Sage tincture has antifungal and antibacterial properties, making it effective in treating dandruff and itchy scalp. Nettle tincture is rich in vitamins and minerals that can help to strengthen hair and reduce hair loss. It is important to note that while herbal tinctures can be effective in treating these conditions, it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before starting any new treatment.
